blurb: Flat oval pads sprouting in pairs like bunny ears, dotted with tufts of fine golden glochids instead of long spines. Cute — but those tufts are the real spines here, and they mean it.

care.light: Full sun for compact pads and to keep new growth from stretching; it's a true desert cactus and loves all the light it can get.
care.water: Soak-and-dry — it takes a bit more water than a globular cactus while actively growing pads in summer, then stays dry through winter.
care.temperature: Warm in summer; keep above ~35°F and dry when cold.
care.dormancy: A summer grower; rest cool and dry in winter.
care.feeding: A dilute cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Repot with tongs or folded newspaper — never bare hands. Pads snap off easily as new plants.
care.propagation: Lay a detached pad on dry mix to callus, then pot — it roots readily.
care.bloom: Yellow flowers along the pad edges on mature plants in strong light.
care.troubles: The glochids are the main hazard to you; the plant itself mainly fears overwatering rot and cochineal scale (white cottony spots).
care.toxicity: Non-toxic to eat, but the tiny barbed glochids lodge in skin and are miserable to remove — keep it where pets and people won't brush it.

sources.title: Opuntia microdasys (Bunny Ears Cactus) — NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox
